bbc.title: Trump's theatrical State of the Union address offers little hint of any change in course
bbc.description: The BBC's Anthony Zurcher says the US president appealed to his base and taunted his opponents in a marathon speech.

bbc.title: Threat of further violence looms after Mexican cartel rampage

bbc.title: Bill Gates 'took responsibility' over Epstein ties in staff meeting, foundation says
bbc.description: A newspaper reports Gates apologised to staff over his links to Epstein and said: "I did nothing illicit. I saw nothing illicit."

bbc.title: Two charged with murder of Australian grandfather after 'mistaken identity' kidnapping
bbc.description: Chris Baghsarian, 85, was taken from his home and murdered, in what authorities believe was a case of mistaken identity.

bbc.title: Japan to deploy missiles on island near Taiwan by 2031
bbc.description: The remote Yonaguni island lies on the frontlines of self-governing Taiwan's growing tensions with China.

bbc.title: Zimbabwe rejects 'lopsided' US health aid deal over data concerns
bbc.description: Access to biological samples raised flags for Zimbabwe as the US did not want to share the benefits of its research.

bbc.title: Don't break up NewJeans and I'll forgo $18m payout, says ex-K-pop boss
bbc.description: Min Hee-jin has said she "can no longer bear to watch" the group get "torn apart".

bbc.title: Senegal PM proposes tougher anti-LGBT law, doubling prison terms
bbc.description: The bill seeks prison terms of five to ten years for what it describes as "acts against nature".

wsj.title: Palestinians Stream Back to Northern Gaza on Foot
wsj.description: Israel allowed displaced Gazans to begin crossing a military zone that bisects the enclave after a deadlock over hostage releases was broken.

wsj.title: Suspected Sabotage of Deep-Sea Cable Triggers First NATO-Led Response

wsj.title: Rwanda-Backed Rebels Enter Congo's Safe-Haven City
wsj.description: Residents of Goma reported gunfire and shelling after rebels overran Congolese troops. U.N. officials estimated that more than one million displaced people were now inside the city.

wsj.title: Cocaine-Funded Gangs Shake Colombia Years After Peace Pact
wsj.description: Attacks by powerful militias against civilians reflect the state’s inaction eight years after a peace accord removed a powerful rebel group from the field.

wsj.title: Stocks Sink in Broad AI Rout Sparked by China's DeepSeek
wsj.description: U.S. stocks were mostly lower, with the Nasdaq leading declines as makers of AI infrastructure suffered steep falls, many in the double digits. Nvidia was down 16%.
